hook.text: Which migration has been applied? A schema_migrations table tracks every version that ran successfully.

buildup.text: Each migration gets a unique version: a timestamp or sequence number. Applied migrations are recorded.

discovery.text: Run 'migrate up': pending migrations apply. Run 'migrate status': see what's applied and what's pending.

twist.text: Two developers create migration 005 for different changes. Conflict! Use timestamps instead of sequence numbers.

climax.text: Store migrations in version control alongside app code. The schema and code evolve together.

punchline.text: Track migrations like code commits. Every change is versioned.

quiz.question: Why are timestamps better than sequence numbers for migration versions?
options: They avoid conflicts when multiple developers create migrations at the same time
Sequence numbers are not supported by any migration tool
